title: Carnage on the Estoril Coast
theme: Quinta da Marinha, new to TEG, hands out a collective hiding — Williams survives best to lead the Trophy, Mullin sneaks the Jacket, and Neumann is already 12 clear at the wrong end.
opening_hook: Open on the carnage scoreboard at sundown — six players, one new Robert Trent Jones layout, and a body count of blow-ups — before rewinding to the first tee.
foreshadow: Alex Baker's electric opening — 15 points in five holes (r1_b08) — that the back nine would mug him for (r1_b18, r1_b21)
Henry Meller's 10 at the par-5 3rd (r1_b06), the day's first signature disaster, which dropped him to the foot of the Spoon race (r1_b23) before Neumann took it off him
Gregg Williams' birdie at the 10th that grabbed the Trophy lead (r1_b10, r1_b26) — a high-water mark he then promptly leaked back through holes 12-15 (r1_b22)

competition_state.name: Trophy
competition_state.note: Williams seized the Stableford lead with his birdie at the 10th (r1_b26) and, despite a sloppy 12-15 stretch that bled 10 shots (r1_b22), held off Jon Baker by four. First round, first race — but with the field spread from 38 down to 17, the gaps are already meaningful.

competition_state.name: Green Jacket
competition_state.note: The Gross lead changed hands repeatedly on the back nine (r1_b30, r1_b31, r1_b32); Mullin moved clear with a par at the 13th (r1_b27) and clung on by a single shot from Jon Baker, with Williams five back.

competition_state.name: Wooden Spoon
competition_state.note: Meller briefly owned the basement after his 10 at the 3rd (r1_b23), but Neumann took it back at the 4th (r1_b24) and then spent holes 7-15 cementing it, bleeding 29 shots in nine holes (r1_b01) en route to his worst round to date (r1_b07). Twelve points clear of the field, in the wrong direction.

players.arc: The day's best survivor — surged through holes 8-11 (r1_b10) and a birdie at the 10th to grab the Trophy lead (r1_b26), then leaked it back through a 10-shot wobble at 12-15 (r1_b22) but still finished four clear on Stableford.
Quietly second in both races despite a 9 of his own at the 13th (r1_b03); steadied himself with a birdie at the 17th (r1_b19) to stay within four of Williams and one of Mullin.

The round's tragic arc — 15 points in the first five holes (r1_b08), then unravelled with quadruple bogeys at 8, 10, 12 and 18 (r1_b11, r1_b12, r1_b13, r1_b14) and a 9 at the 17th (r1_b02), the 14-shot back-nine bleed (r1_b18) leaving a 10-point split between his nines (r1_b17).
Took the Green Jacket lead with a par at the 13th (r1_b27) and held it by a shot, despite his own cold patch at 6-8 (r1_b25); tied with Alex Baker in the Stableford on 30.

A 10 at the par-5 3rd (r1_b06) was the day's first disaster, briefly sinking him to the foot of the Spoon (r1_b23) before Neumann relieved him; a birdie at the 5th stopped the bleeding (r1_b20), but another 6-shot bleed at 13-15 (r1_b29) capped a 29-point round.

A grim afternoon — the Wooden Spoon was his by the 4th (r1_b24) and never threatened to leave, with 9s at the 10th, 11th and 12th (r1_b15, r1_b16, r1_b04), an 8 at the 14th (r1_b05) and 29 shots bled across nine holes (r1_b01) sealing his worst TEG round to date (r1_b07).

venue_notes: Quinta da Marinha — a new course for TEG (visit_n 1), a Robert Trent Jones Sr. parkland on the Estoril Coast with elevated targets and abundant bunkering. TEG's second visit to the Lisbon Coast. Lean into the unfamiliarity: the elevated targets and the bunkering did much of the day's damage.
